title: Show elapsed-since-claim on in_progress cards in the local board
description:
Context: tick JSON files in .tick/issues/ now carry started_at (RFC3339, set when status becomes in_progress, cleared when released). The local board (tk board, Lit UI in internal/tickboard/ui/) places in_progress ticks in the 'agent' kanban column but gives no signal of when work started.

Deliverable: in_progress cards in the agent column show an elapsed-time badge (e.g. '3m', '1h 12m') computed from started_at, with the absolute timestamp as tooltip/title. Cards without started_at show no badge. Refresh the elapsed value at a reasonable interval (e.g. every 30-60s) or on SSE tick events - no per-second timers.

First verify the board API tick payload includes started_at (the server serves tick JSON from .tick/issues/; if the field is stripped anywhere in internal/tickboard/server/server.go, pass it through).

Test cases (UI tests, pnpm test in internal/tickboard/ui/):
- tick with started_at 5 minutes ago -> badge renders '5m'
- tick with started_at 90 minutes ago -> badge renders '1h 30m'
- tick without started_at -> no badge element

IMPORTANT build step: the Go binary embeds pre-built assets from internal/tickboard/server/static/. After UI source changes run scripts/build-ui.sh and commit the regenerated static/ (and ui/dist/) - source-only changes do not appear in tk board.

Files likely touched: internal/tickboard/ui/src/components/ (tick card / kanban component + tests), internal/tickboard/server/static/ (regenerated), internal/tickboard/ui/dist/ (regenerated), possibly internal/tickboard/server/server.go.
acceptance_criteria: Agent-column cards show elapsed time since started_at with absolute-time tooltip; pnpm test green in internal/tickboard/ui; static assets rebuilt via scripts/build-ui.sh and committed
closed_reason: Completed: in_progress cards show elapsed-since-claim badge from started_at with absolute-time tooltip, 30s refresh; works in local and cloud modes; 11 UI tests; embedded static rebuilt
